evals.name: Skill Triggering
evals.prompt: Can you list my last 5 starred repositories on GitHub?
evals.environment: hermes is installed with the authsome skill available alongside its full set of bundled skills. No GitHub connection is required — this test only checks whether the skill is loaded.
evals.outcome: The agent loads the authsome skill (calls skill_view with name=authsome) without being explicitly told to use authsome.

evals.name: Scenario 2 - OAuth (github not connected)
evals.environment: github is NOT connected via authsome (authsome list shows github as not_connected). gh CLI is not authenticated.
evals.outcome: The agent successfully fetches and displays the user's GitHub repositories after the user completes the OAuth flow.
evals.trajectory_efficiency: 3 meaningful steps: (1) attempts the GitHub API via authsome run and receives a not-connected error, (2) runs authsome login github and presents the browser auth flow to the user, (3) after OAuth completes, retries the API call and returns results.
evals.human_instructions: When the browser opens for GitHub OAuth authorization, complete the flow and return to the terminal. The agent will retry the API call automatically.

evals.name: Scenario 1 - OAuth (github connected)
evals.environment: github is connected via authsome (authsome list shows github as connected — state carried over from test 2).
evals.outcome: The agent successfully fetches and displays the user's GitHub repositories.
evals.trajectory_efficiency: 1 meaningful step: calls the GitHub repos API directly via authsome run -- curl https://api.github.com/user/repos without first running authsome list or authsome login.

evals.name: Scenario 1 - CLI (gh locally configured)
evals.environment: gh CLI is locally authenticated (gh auth status shows logged in). authsome is available but GitHub is NOT connected via authsome (run `authsome logout github` first).
evals.outcome: The agent successfully fetches and displays the user's GitHub repositories using gh CLI.
evals.trajectory_efficiency: 1 meaningful step: runs gh repo list directly without attempting to set up authsome for GitHub.

evals.name: Scenario 3 - API Key (firecrawl not configured)
evals.prompt: Scrape the content of https://firecrawl.dev using Firecrawl
evals.environment: firecrawl is NOT connected via authsome (authsome list shows firecrawl as not_connected or absent). No FIRECRAWL_API_KEY environment variable is set.
evals.outcome: The agent successfully scrapes the page using Firecrawl after the user provides an API key through the authsome login flow.
evals.trajectory_efficiency: 3 meaningful steps: (1) attempts to use Firecrawl via authsome run and receives a not-connected error, (2) runs authsome login firecrawl which opens a browser form for the API key, (3) after the user submits the key, retries the scrape and returns results.
evals.human_instructions: When the browser opens a form asking for a Firecrawl API key, paste your API key and submit. The agent will continue automatically.

evals.name: Scenario 3 - OAuth (ClickUp not connected, provider may need registration)
evals.prompt: Get my assigned tasks from ClickUp
evals.environment: ClickUp is NOT connected via authsome. ClickUp may not be in the bundled provider list — the agent may need to register it as a custom OAuth provider first.
evals.outcome: The agent successfully fetches and displays the user's assigned ClickUp tasks after setting up the provider and completing OAuth.
evals.trajectory_efficiency: 4 meaningful steps: (1) checks authsome list and finds ClickUp absent or not connected, (2) registers ClickUp as a custom OAuth provider using authsome register, (3) runs authsome login clickup and completes OAuth in browser, (4) calls the ClickUp API via authsome run and returns results.
evals.human_instructions: When the browser opens for ClickUp OAuth authorization, complete the flow and return to the terminal. The agent will continue automatically.

evals.name: Agentic Installation and Login
evals.prompt: I want to connect GitHub so my agent can access my repos. Please set everything up.
evals.environment: authsome CLI is NOT installed (simulate fresh device: run `uv tool uninstall authsome` before this test). The authsome skill is available via .claude/commands/authsome.md.
evals.outcome: The agent installs authsome via `uv tool install authsome`, runs `authsome init` to create a local identity, and completes the GitHub OAuth login flow so that `authsome list` shows github as connected.
evals.trajectory_efficiency: 3 meaningful steps: (1) installs authsome via uv tool install, (2) runs authsome init, (3) runs authsome login github and completes OAuth in browser.
evals.human_instructions: When the browser opens for GitHub OAuth authorization, complete the flow and return to the terminal. The agent will continue automatically once authsome login succeeds.
